summary: Material authorization and SSRF gaps found in the reviewed server surfaces.

findings.invariant: (1) authorization/tenant-isolation bypass - a route or data access that returns/mutates another org's or another user's resource without a clean 403/404 (org derived from the authenticated principal, NEVER a request body/param the client controls)
findings.title: Job SSE stream attaches to any job id without checking job ownership
findings.recommendation: Before `sseManager.attach`, load the job with `getJob`, require `job.userId === auth.claims.sub` or an authorized admin role, and return 404/403 on mismatch, mirroring the guarded `GET /:id` route.

findings.invariant: (2) injection - command (bash/exec), template ({{}} expansion reaching a shell/eval), SQL, or SSRF (an outbound fetch to a client-controlled URL not passing the SSRF guard)
findings.title: PDF render uses client-controlled Host header as a browser navigation target
findings.recommendation: Do not derive the render origin from `req.get('host')`. Use a configured internal/public origin allowlist or a fixed loopback origin for served apps, and reject untrusted Host values before invoking `renderArtifactPdf`.

findings.invariant: (2) injection - command (bash/exec), template ({{}} expansion reaching a shell/eval), SQL, or SSRF (an outbound fetch to a client-controlled URL not passing the SSRF guard)
findings.title: App builder fetches arbitrary HTTP import URLs without the SSRF guard
findings.recommendation: Replace the raw `fetch` with `guardedFetch` and restrict build-time remote module fetching to known CDN hosts; reject unknown HTTP(S) import URLs instead of fetching them directly.

findings.invariant: (2) injection - command (bash/exec), template ({{}} expansion reaching a shell/eval), SQL, or SSRF (an outbound fetch to a client-controlled URL not passing the SSRF guard)
findings.title: DNS rebinding check misses IPv4-mapped private IPv6 answers
findings.recommendation: Apply the same IPv6 and IPv4-mapped IPv6 blocking logic used by `url-safety.ts` to resolved DNS answers, including `::`, `::ffff:10/8`, `::ffff:169.254/16`, `::ffff:172.16/12`, and `::ffff:192.168/16`.
